sections.content: New state 17! Let me check what changed. The image shows the orange object now has a white diamond next to it on the right side. Let me check the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== Key colored centers ===")
for r in range(len(ascii_grid)):
    for c in range(len(ascii_grid[r])):
        ch = ascii_grid[r][c]
        if ch in 'MO':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): {ch}")

print("\n=== White pixels ===")
for r in range(len(ascii_grid)):
    for c in range(len(ascii_grid[r])):
        if ascii_grid[r][c] == 'w':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): w")

# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Changed cells ===")
for r in range(len(after)):
    for c in range(len(after[r])):
        if r < len(before) and c < len(before[r]) and after[r][c] != before[r][c]:
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): {before[r][c]} -> {after[r][c]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The orange object is still at (12-17, 51-55) with M at (15, 53). The white pixels are the same: (17, 55), etc.
But there are changes:
- White pixels at (10, 51), (11, 50-52), (12, 49-50, 52-53), (13, 50-51) turned gray (W→G) - this is the white diamond at the top turning back to gray
- Gray pixels at (16, 57), (17, 56-58), (18, 55-56, 58-59), (19, 56-58), (20, 57) turned white (G→W) - this is a new white diamond appearing near the orange object at (18, 57)
Looking at the image, I can see a white diamond with an orange center at (18, 57). The orange object is now next to this white diamond.
The pattern seems to be:
1. Move the orange object along the path
2. When it reaches a white diamond, the diamond turns gray (completing it)
3. A new white diamond appears at the next position
4. Continue until all diamonds are completed
But wait, the white diamond at the top turned from white to gray. That's the opposite of what I expected. Let me reconsider.
Actually, looking at the changes:
- The white diamond at the top (which I clicked earlier) turned back to gray
- A new white diamond appeared at (16-20, 55-59) with center at (18, 57)
So the pattern might be:
1. Click white pixels to move the orange object
2. When the orange object reaches a gray diamond, it turns white
3. When you click the white diamond's center, it turns gray (completing it)
4. A new white diamond appears at the next position
But I didn't click the white diamond's center, I clicked (17, 55) which is a white pixel near the orange object. Let me check if the orange object moved to the white diamond.
Actually, looking at the image, the orange object is still at (12-17, 51-55). The white diamond is at (16-20, 55-59) with center at (18, 57). The orange object hasn't reached the white diamond yet.
I think I need to click the orange object's M center at (15, 53) to move it toward the white diamond.
